# Project Guidelines

## Code Style
- Use Spanish for comments and variable names in exercises (e.g., `#escribimos un párrafo`).
- HTML files: Basic HTML5 structure with semantic elements.
- Python files: Standard library only, informal code for learning.

## Architecture
- Workspace divided into HTLM/ (front-end basics) and Python/ (back-end fundamentals).
- Exercises are standalone, no dependencies or integrations.
- Progressive learning: Numbered folders indicate increasing complexity.

## Build and Test
- No build system. Run Python scripts individually: `python <filename>.py`
- Open HTML files in browser: `open index.html` or use browser.
- Ensure Python 3.x is available; update hardcoded paths in scripts to match current user.

## Conventions
- File naming: Numbered prefixes (e.g., `01_primerospasos.py`) for sequencing.
- HTML: `index.html` and `estilos.css` (Spanish for styles).
- Update absolute paths in Python scripts (e.g., replace `/Users/emfashiondesign/` with current user path).
- See theory docs: [HTLM/TEORÍA/Organigrama HTML CSS.pages](HTLM/TEORÍA/Organigrama HTML CSS.pages) and [Python/3_Teoría/PYTHON TEORÍA.pages](Python/3_Teoría/PYTHON TEORÍA.pages) for concepts.</content>
